title: asperula nitida subsp. hirtella
intro: Asperula nitida subsp. hirtella, also known as Woodruff, is a perennial herb belonging to the Rubiaceae family. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of digestive issues, such as diarrhea and stomach cramps, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is also believed to have various health benefits, including reducing stress and anxiety, improving sleep quality, and possessing antioxidant properties. Asperula nitida subsp. hirtella can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is sometimes used in traditional medicine to aid in digestion and alleviate symptoms of various gastrointestinal disorders.
